briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the MST/SST example using the XDpTxSs driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if DisplayPort TX Subsystem configured in SST/MST successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if DisplayPort TX Subsystem failed to configure in SST/MST.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform-specifc peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function setup stream source to input DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para:
specifies whether to split video frame vertically into two different vertical halves.
1 = Vertically split input frame
0 = No vertically split input frame.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if stream source is configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XDpTxSs SST/MST example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the MST/SST example passed.
XST_FAILURE if the MST/SST example was unsuccessful.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XDpTxSs driver in single stream (SST) transport or multi-stream transport (MST) mode.

simplesect.para: For this example to display output, the user need to implement initialization of the system (DpTxSs_PlatformInit) and after DisplayPort TX subsystem start (XDpTxSs_Start) is complete, implement configuration of the video stream source in order to provide the DisplayPort TX Subsystem HIP input. The functions DpTxSs_PlatformInit and DpTxSs_StreamSrc are declared and are left up to the user implement.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 07/01/15 Initial release. 4.1 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
